channel.title: bas_ — dispatches
channel.description: Long-form notes on systems, software, and things worth measuring.

item.title: On the quiet cost of context switches, and the places they hide
item.description: A context switch costs a few microseconds on paper. In practice, it costs a cache, a branch predictor, and the quiet coherence you'd built up over the last thousand instructions. A walk through perf, some toy benchmarks, and the strange kind of patience required to measure what isn't there.

item.title: Entropy to Order: How I Rebuilt My Second Brain in Mordor
item.description: I had a thousand bookmarks and zero ideas. This is the long story of how I tore the whole thing down, named the new system Mordor, and started writing notes that actually argue with me.
